qas.a: Records Management Program legal obligation to maintain AF records
from creation to disposition

qas.q: Air Force chief information officer ( SAF/A6 & CIO )
qas.a:
Overall responsibility for the AFrecords management program.
Develops policies + guidelines.

Administers the AF management program for the CIO
Represents the SAF on records management issues
Develops + issues life cycle policies of AF records
Publishes Records Disposition schedule

qas.q: Command records manager ( CRM ) and Agency records manager ( ARM )
qas.a:
Appointed by the Majcom Commander
Manage AF records management programs within the command' Agency
Develops and provides a training program within their organization at base level

Ensure implementation of AF records management with their organization at base levels!
Appoint the BRM to manage at least a GS9 or E-7 (MSgt)

Administer the installation records management program
All AF data calls/records searches as applicable
Develop the basis Vital Records and Disaster + Recovery plan

qas.q: BRM duties fall under three categories they are. . .
qas.a: Training, Assistance and managing staging areas
BRM will train the FARM, COR, and RC, within 3 months (6 for ANG)
Assisits the Farm, COR, and RC on any records
Managing a staging area for temporary storage of records pending destruction or transfer to a federal records center

Serves as the POC
Monitor's the program
Serves as the liaison
Squadron staff assistant visit ever 24 months
SR-A (lvl 5) or GS-05
Must take role based FARM training annually

qas.q: Chief of an office of record
qas.a:
Highest ranking person in the office
Physical possession of records
* Appoints the RC+ ensures RC has training
*Approves the File Plan (cover sheet)*
Takes COR training annually
*Responsible for physical and legal custody of all records appoints a RC

Maintains services and disposes office records
Lowest level on the hierarchy but by far the most important player.
Must take role based RC training annually
RC builds the office file plan

Users are ignorant
Protect fouo pa and pii
all AF personnel
Comply and respond to the FARM / RC

All books, papers maps charts or other documentary materials. Signed and unsigned e-mails reflecting business decisions, decision memos sent via web - mail documents authenticated at workflow, emails sent as actions,taskers, policies and information, information / records maintained in a system of record, guides, plans, pamphlets, handbooks and continuity books, memos and forms!
active files, cutoff, data call, disposition, records disposition instructions, File, Inactive files, Office of records
A plan designating the physical locations where the agencys files will be maintained, the specific types of files and the orginazational elements having custodial responsibility
active files are the records an office uses frequently in current business or until a cutoff period is met

A term used for separating active records and inactive records
-Monthly
-fiscal year (30 sep)
-calendar year ( 31 Dec )
-None

requests for records (ie record search). Normally a (blank) is a request for all active,inactive physical and electronic records, final documental materials ( includes unclassified and classified and drafts, emails) in response to a non-FOIA or FOIA request

The actions taken regarding records no longer needed for current government business including transfer to agency storage facilities or FRC, transfer from one federal agency to another :transfer of permanent records to the national archives, and disposal of temporary records

A document providing mandatory instructions for what to do with records (and non records) no longer needed for current government business, with provision of authority for the final disposition of recurring or nonrecurring records
Usually an accumulation of records the officer no longer needs or in a retention period after the cutoff date and awaiting final disposition. These are normally files from the previous year.
Inactive files are records the office no longer needs ir in a retention period after the cutoff date and awaiting final disposition. These are normally files from the previous year
the office that is designated by the directorate record officer as responsible for the custody, maintenance and retirement or disposal of the records it holds
The transfer of records to a federal records center (FRC)

SAV is a checklist that can be performed at least every 24 months by either the BRM or FARM to ensure that the COR and RC are following instructions properly and maintaining files within standards. It also provides an opportunity for the RC's to ask for assistance from the FARM's with records issues. The SAV is a scheduled inspection. the BRM or FARM

the program manager and the office of primary responsibility for planning,managing and facilitating the records management program and related policies . qas.q: The RDS screen is broken out like this pg 25 top
qas.a:
Left side top left = Table and Rule, Title, Authority, Current
Right side top right = Date created,date modified ,date approved , frozen record
A record that is suspended from its rule of destruction. Frozen records are identified by the CRM/BRM

qas.q: The RDS page is broken out like this pg 25 bottom
qas.a:
Column B -explains what the record consist of
column C- explains what the records are for
Column D Disposition -explains the disposition instructions, which is also where you get your cut off
allows the classification of each record and provides a tool for creation of a standardized plan, its a software program mandatory for AF managers and personell on how to store files,

qas.a: T 37-19 Files maintenance and disposition (media neutral)

qas.q: AFRIMS File plan is met so the rc does a file plan approval and is considered approved when
qas.a: The records managers apporoval + the chief of an office of record signs appears on the file plan cover sheet and they send a courtesy copy

Farm =Functional Area Records Manager
+performs supervisory duties in unit or functional area
+ serves as the POC and monitors the Records management program,
+ Assists the COR and RC in maintain and disposing records.
+Only one Farm is authorized per unit functional area (pg 4)

COR= Chief of an office of Record
+ one individual authorized per flight or individual office level
+ Responsible for the records under their scope of control
+ Has physical and legal custody of records
+Appoints the RC and ensure the RC attends management training

RC=Records Custodian
+maintain,services, and disposes of office records
+Maintains office file plan and accountabilliy for an active and inactive records ensures eligibable records are promptly prepared.

qas.q: Overview of the CRM and ARM
qas.a:
CRM= Command response manager,
ARM = Agency Records Manager
+appointed by the MAJCOM Commander
+ manage AF Records Management programs within their Command/Agency
+Develop and provide a training program for newly appointed Base Records Manager

qas.q: File plan Approval process for RC
qas.a:
RC conduct a weekly review of file plans at a minimum, they check a box select "request approval" and then press send! extra this action causes AFIRMS to send emails to the TO and CC emails will also be sent to BRMs and FARMs.
BRMs= Base Records Manager
FARMs= Functional Area Records Manager

qas.q: what two conditions must be met for the AFIRMS file plan to be approved?
qas.a:
1. records manger's administrative approval (using AFIRMS) is stated on the file plan.
2.) The COR signature appears on the file plan cover sheet
COR=Chief of an office of record

qas.a:
1.) AF-wide
2.) MAJCOM

this is a special duty for record search

qas.q: Searches must be completed in an extremely time- comstrained and prompt manner who moniters all the searches...

The CRM and ARM Command records manager and
Agency records manager they report to the AFRO

qas.q: AFRO provides a summary report of the search results what are the two types of responses ...?
qas.a:
Negative - provide date location, time it tooke, and duration of search
Positive response - date location, search was accomplished, first last grade dsn phone , select media type

qas.a:
Records must be put into "litigation hold"- held either in the orgs area and marked accordingly
Records can also be assigned "Frozen Records

prepare a list of all individuals
segregrate and mark documents
search results will flow from RC to COR to FARM to BRM to CRM to AFRO
"Road map" or "table of contents"for office use

folders that support the primary reference. Subdivisions are optional but ease the filing process

positioned on disposition guide cards (primary folder)
are positioned on sub divisions folders

books papers, maps , photographs, electronic media that AF receive

enduring value because they document the organizations

qas.q: records staging is required for records where is it?
qas.a: RDS staging areas in the put it in a disposition bin in the RC's office or in a local staging area ( the BRM's staging area) or sent to approved MAJCOM records manager staging area ,NARA,

qas.q: staging request are in AFRIMS the facility retains a period of 8 years or less
qas.a: The Afrims staging module is an automated management processs which allows record professionals to prepare standard form 135s (records transmittal and receipt)- anything getting retired
sf allows staging approval, staging requests ,delivery to nara, or frc! SF 135 is initiated regardless of media located in AFFRIMS in STAGING tab
ERM solutions guide

qas.q: Paper records must meet NARA approved requirements which are
qas.a:
records physically prepared paperclips, rubber bands removed
Appropriate NARA approved containers used to house record
Appropriate markings
Charge out record form for removing files

qas.q: electronic record being staged requires an SF 135 to be completed
qas.a: TRUE

qas.q: 1. What is the purpose of AFRIMS?
qas.a: Allows for the classification of each record (regardless of media) and provides a tool for creation of a standardized plan of how each record an office maintains is kept and in what format

qas.q: 2. How many different sections are there in AFRIMS?
qas.a: Seven

qas.q: 3. Name some of the different sections within AFRIMS.
qas.a: Menu bar, system notices, announcements, your base records managers, tasks, recent activity and resources

qas.q: 4. A records professional could look in the "Tasks" section for information about a Data Call being due.
qas.a: True

qas.q: 5. What do the CRM/ARM, BRM, FARM, COR and RC have a responsibility to do in relation to AFRIMS?
qas.a: All of these key players must complete "annual" training based on each role they reside in, to maintain AFRIMS accesses

qas.q: 1. What is the purpose of records?
qas.a: They document official business, keep an account of an organization's activities, historical analysis and the basis for future actions
Chief of an Office of Record - COR

qas.q: 3.) Who ensures that the Records Custodian attends records management training?
qas.a: Chief of an Office of Record - COR

qas.q: 4.) Who administers the records management program on an installation or base?
qas.a: Base Records Manager - RM

qas.q: 5.) What are the three main responsibilities of the Base Records Manager?
qas.a:
Provide Assistance
Managing Staging Areas
Training
Federal, Personal, Permanent, Temporary, State, Records in Other Than Paper Form, Vital Records, Perpetual, Final and Draft records

qas.q: 1.)What is the purpose of the Files Maintenance and Disposition Plan?
qas.a: Air Force units at all levels have a requirement to document their organization's functions, policies, procedures and activities.

qas.q: 2.) What are the steps to process and produce the File Plan?
qas.a:
Step 1- Capture Records
Step 2- Identify Records
Step 3- Populate File Plan

qas.q: 3.) What is the first file that needs to be apart of the content of the File Plan?
qas.a: T 37 - 19 R 02.00 Files Maintenance and Disposition (Media Neutral)

qas.q: 4.) Any records professional can approve the file plan in AFRIMS?
qas.a: False

qas.q: 5.) In AFRIMS, You can only print the file plan.
qas.a: False

qas.q: 6.) The Acronym RDS stands for________ and what is the purpose?
qas.a:
Records Disposition Schedule
To help with the identification of a record, so it can be properly classified under the correct table and rule (T&R) and to ensure the record is kept according to its proper and required life cycle and NOT destroyed prior to its allowable destruction timeline

qas.q: 1.) What is the purpose of the "RECORD SEARCH" tab in AFRIMS?
qas.a: It is where you locate the records search parameters and input the positive or negative response info.

qas.q: 2.) What is another name for a Records Search?
qas.a: Data Call

qas.q: 3.) What is the routing chain of a Records Search?
qas.a: AFRO to CRM to BRM to FARM to RC/COR

qas.q: 4.) Records Searches are not a mandatory requirement
qas.a: False

qas.q: 5.) Records Searches are not a mandatory requirement.
qas.a: False

qas.q: 6.)Records preservation can include records being put into _________ and ___________ status.
qas.a: Litigation Hold and Frozen Record

qas.q: 7.) Close out of the Records search contains the following information to be reported in AFRIMS.
qas.a: List of the all individuals, grade, duty location, email address, telephone numbers and the estimated man hours (authorizations, rank or grade, and hours spent) to conduct each search regardless of whether or not documents are found.

qas.q: 1.) What is the purpose of the AF Form 614 and 614a?
qas.a: It is used as a Charge Out Record form for removing files from paper file cabinet

qas.q: 2.) What guidance governs Record Charge Out procedures?
qas.a: AFMAN 33-363, Chapter 3

qas.q: 3.) What information is annotated on the AF Form 614 or 614a?
qas.a:
Date of Document
Title of Document, File Code
Name, Office Title or Symbol, and Telephone Number
Date document/file is charged out

qas.q: 4.) . Electronic record being staged requires an SF Form 135 to be completed.
qas.a: True

qas.q: 5.) What is an example of an approved off-site records center?
qas.a: MAJCOM Records Manager Staging Areas for holding or forwarding to the Federal Records Center, NARA (National Archives and Records Administration).

qas.q: 6.) Records preservation for staging includes records being physically _______ for staging and put in _________.
qas.a: Prepared/ NARA Approved containers
